name: Augustus
note: Born 23 September 63 BCE and died 19 August 14 CE; current ruler at the AD 1 start.

name: Livia
note: Augustus's wife; dates use the conventional modern chronology.

name: Gaius Caesar
note: Gaius is the designated heir in AD 1; his birth day is not securely attested and 1 January is an explicit technical convention.

name: Tiberius
note: Present as a court figure only: the generator must not make him heir or adopted before AD 4.

name: Grand Empress Dowager Wang
note: Her birth year is conventionally given as 71 BCE; 1 January is an explicit technical convention pending a dedicated Han prosopography pass.

name: Emperor Ping
note: The child emperor reigning at the AD 1 start; death date follows the standard Han chronology.

name: Wang Mang
note: Regent in AD 1 and future usurper; the birth year is attested but day and month are not, so 1 January is an explicit technical convention.

name: Musa
note: Queen-mother and co-ruler; the available source does not support a precise biographical date, so none is scripted.

name: Phraates V
note: Arsacid King of Kings in AD 1; source bounds his accession and reign but does not justify fabricated day-level biography dates.

name: Maroboduus
note: King of the Marcomanni at the AD 1 start; no day-level biography date is scripted.

name: Arminius
note: Cheruscan aristocrat recorded as serving in the Roman military before the Teutoburg situation; not a Roman ruler or heir.

name: Juba II
note: Roman client king of Mauretania at the AD 1 start; no fabricated day-level biography date.

name: Cleopatra Selene II
note: Ptolemaic queen at the Mauretanian court; represented with her own dynasty rather than assimilating her to Juba's line.

name: Aretas IV
note: Nabataean king at the AD 1 start; no day-level biography date is scripted.

name: Natakamani
note: Kushite king at the AD 1 start and the named co-ruler in the plan's roster.

name: Amanitore
note: Kushite kandake and co-ruler; the engine consort slot is a technical representation and does not assert an unresolved personal relationship.

name: Wuzhuliu Chanyu
note: Xiongnu chanyu at the AD 1 start; no day-level biography date is scripted.

name: Yuri of Goguryeo
note: Second Goguryeo king whose conventional reign spans 19 BCE to AD 18.

name: Tasciovanus
note: Catuvellaunian ruler at the AD 1 start; the plan reserves Cunobelinus's prominence for the later succession.

name: Cunobelinus
note: Catuvellaunian successor figure represented as the named heir without an invented day-level biography date.

name: Herod Archelaus
note: Herodian ethnarch of Judea and Samaria at the AD 1 start; distinct from the Cappadocian king of the same name.

name: Herod Antipas
note: Herodian tetrarch of Galilee and Peraea at the AD 1 start.

name: Philip the Tetrarch
note: Herodian tetrarch of Batanea and the northern territories at the AD 1 start.
